findings.issue: Edge case section documents 'gasPrice === 0n' as the condition to detect unavailable gas pricing, but in ethers v6, provider.getFeeData() returns null (not 0n) for gasPrice on EIP-1559 networks. An executor following this description will implement a check for 0n and miss the null case, causing a TypeError when computing 'gasCostWei = gasEstimate * gasPrice' if gasPrice is null.
findings.fix: Change the edge case text to: 'if (feeData.gasPrice === null || feeData.gasPrice === 0n)' — check for null first (ethers v6 FeeData.gasPrice is typed as bigint | null). Surface 'gas pricing unavailable' via the notes field and skip the threshold assertion.

findings.issue: Description line 27 says the root workspaces array must list 'contracts' (alongside 'packages/*, apps/*, scripts as established by Task 1'). Task 1 establishes workspaces: ["packages/*", "apps/*", "contracts"] — it never adds 'scripts' as a workspace entry. The word 'scripts' in the alongside clause is incorrect; scripts/ is a top-level CLI directory, not an npm workspace.
findings.fix: Change line 27 to: 'alongside packages/*, apps/*, contracts as established by Task 1' — remove 'scripts'. The AC (line 61) is already correct and does not mention scripts; only the Description sentence is wrong.

findings.issue: tech-spec.md still places Task 3 under the section header '### Wave 1 — Project setup (parallel)' (tech-spec line 541), but T3's task file correctly sets wave: 2 and depends_on: [2], consistent with iteration-2 systemic-fix §1. The tech-spec wave classification is stale and contradicts the task file. This does not affect execution (task file is authoritative) but will confuse readers who rely on the tech-spec wave table.
findings.fix: Update tech-spec.md: move Task 3 entry to a new '### Wave 2 — Spike (after Wave 1)' section, or add a note that Task 3 was reclassified to Wave 2 by systemic-fix §1. The task body already says 'Wave 2 spike' — the section header is the only discrepancy.

findings.issue: T3 Description attributes the 5% gas-cost threshold to 'external-analysis.md' (line 27: 'per-payment economics threshold from external-analysis.md'), and the Context Files annotation echoes this ('5% gas-cost threshold and per-payment economics rationale'). However, external-analysis.md does not contain the 0.0005 USDC / 5% threshold or the 0.01 USDC reference price — those come from tech-spec.md Risks table (line 487: 'If gas > 5% of a 0.01 USDC payment'). external-analysis.md only contains general arguments about economic floor without specific percentage thresholds.
findings.fix: Change the attribution: 'gas cost > 0.0005 USDC (5% of a 0.01 USDC payment — per tech-spec Risks row: Per-payment settlement creates per-event gas overhead)'. Update the Context Files annotation for external-analysis.md to reflect its actual content: 'per-request economics rationale (no specific % threshold)'. The threshold value itself is correct throughout T3.
